text:
Market today: Mixed open to Q3 with rotation away from momentum names - chips specifically getting hit (Sandisk/Micron sinking per headlines). That's the real driver for your book today; semis under pressure while broader market muddles through.

Your holdings: SOXL (-13.78%) is your loudest problem - 3x levered semis on a day chips are rotating out, and you're already deep underwater with margin amplifying every tick down; stop at $200 is your line. SPAL (-13.36%) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO-adjacent leveraged ETF moving fast against you - stop at $21.38 is razor-thin and likely getting tested. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down hard with thin liquidity - margin makes this dangerous to hold loose.

Watch: 1) Whether SMH holds above $540 stop as chip rotation accelerates. 2) Friday's June jobs report - a hot number kills the rate-cut narrative and hits QQQ/tech hard.

Bottom line: Your margin exposure in three losing leveraged/spec names during a chip rotation day is the only thing that matters right now - protect capital first.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your risk line - chips rotating out today per headlines, don't let a leveraged 3x position run past it on margin.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is basically at current price ($21.79) - decide now if you're holding through it or cutting; leveraged ETFs don't recover cleanly.
No new buys today - chip rotation headwind is real, jobs report Friday adds uncertainty, and your existing losing positions need attention before adding exposure.
